causeCollection.description:
A lower-level set of structured errors.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

causeIndexedCollection.description:
A lower-level set of structured errors.
Each of these errors MUST have index set.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

causeIsInternal.description: If true, then the cause (`causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection`) should be hidden from the user.

causeSimple.description:
Simple string error type.

Only oneOf `causeWrapped`, `causeSimple`, `causeCollection`, or `causeIndexedCollection` will ever be set.

domain.description:
The "domain" for the error.  If empty, it is an EDA
core error.  Alternatively it can be an EDA application
"apiVersion" value (e.g. interfaces.eda.nokia.com/v1alpha1)
indicating that the error is specific to that application.
The domain gives the receiver information that they can use
to help them interpret the "type" field.

index.description:
When processing an array, errors may be generated related to a particular array item.
If set, this index indicates to which array item the error applies.

message.description: The basic text error message for the error response.

ref.description: Reference to the error source. Should typically be the URI of the request.

type.description:
Type defines a unique identifier for the error, within the domain.
This may be used (along with the domain) to find an internationalization translation for the message.

SHOULD be a valid golang identifier, and SHOULD be in UpperCamelCase.

values.description:
Associated data/information.
The error "message" may contain {{name}} escapes that should be substituted with information from this dictionary.

Note that this map MUST NOT contain JSON objects (`{...}`) or arrays (`[...]`), only simple JSON types are permitted.

ErrorResponse.title: Generic error response for REST APIs.

K8SPatchOp.description: The definition for a JSON patch for a resource, as in RFC 6902

from.description:
Only applies to a "move" operation, and is the path to the field/value within
the target JSON object that should be moved to the location specified by the "path"
field.

op.description: The type of operation this patch should perform.

path.description:
The path to the field within the target JSON object
that should be patched according to the "op" field.

value.description:
This field would be set to the value which the user intends to put in the
field referenced by the "path" field.  The type of the value should match
the type of the field as indicated by the schema of the resource being
patched.

x-permissive.description:
For an "add" operation, an already-present field referenced by the "path" will
result in an error unless this field is true.  Similarly for a non-existent
target field of a "remove" operation.

Status.title: Status is a return value for calls that don't return other objects.

com.nokia.eda.aaa.v1.AuthenticationPolic.description: AuthenticationPolicy is the Schema for the authenticationpolicies API

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-pattern-error: The apiVersion must be set to aaa.eda.nokia.com/v1
The kind must be set to AuthenticationPolicy

spec.description: An AuthenticationPolicy defines parameters that relate to node users, such as password complexity, lockout timers, and the order in which authentication methods are attempted on the node.

exitOnReject.description: When set to True, a reject of an authentication server will result in the user being denied access. When set to False, upon reject the node will try the next authentication method in the authentication order.
exitOnReject.title: Exit on reject

serverGroupOrder.description: List of ServerGroups that define the order in which authentication happens.
serverGroupOrder.title: Server Group Order

idleTimeoutMinutes.description: Determines how long a user can be idle for before they are logged out (in minutes). When set to zero, users are never logged out.
idleTimeoutMinutes.title: Idle timeout

attempts.description: How many failed login attempts are allowed, set to zero for unlimited attempts.
attempts.title: Login attempts

lockoutMinutes.description: How many minutes the user will be locked out for, set to zero for indefinite (until manually unlocked).
lockoutMinutes.title: Lockout time

timeMinutes.description: The time window for failed attempts to occur in (in minutes).
timeMinutes.title: Time window

nodeSelectors.description: Selects TopoNodes on which to configure the AuthenticationPolicy.
nodeSelectors.title: Node Selector

nodes.description: List of TopoNodes on which to configure the AuthenticationPolicy.
nodes.title: Nodes

allowUsername.description: Whether to allow the username to be part of the password.
allowUsername.title: Allow username

maximumSequentialCharacters.description: The maximum length of sequential characters in the password. For example, abcd is a sequence of 4 sequential keys.
maximumSequentialCharacters.title: Maximum number of sequential characters

minimumLength.description: The minimum length of the password.
minimumLength.title: Minimum password length

minimumLowercase.description: The minimum amount of lowercase characters in the password.
minimumLowercase.title: Minimum lowercase characters

minimumNumeric.description: The minimum amount of numeric characters in the password.
minimumNumeric.title: Minimum numeric characters

minimumSpecial.description: The minimum amount of special characters in the password.
minimumSpecial.title: Minimum special characters

minimumUppercase.description: The minimum amount of uppercase characters in the password.
minimumUppercase.title: Minimum uppercase characters

repeatedCharacters.description: How many repeated characters are allowed.
repeatedCharacters.title: Repeated characters

spec.title: Specification

status.description: AuthenticationPolicyStatus defines the observed state of AuthenticationPolicy.

nodes.description: List of nodes this authentication policy has been applied to
nodes.title: Nodes

status.title: Status

com.nokia.eda.aaa.v1.NodeGroup.description: NodeGroup is the Schema for the nodegroups API

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-pattern-error: The apiVersion must be set to aaa.eda.nokia.com/v1
The kind must be set to NodeGroup

spec.description:
NodeGroup is a representation of a group on a node, including the services it has access to, any RBAC, and TACACS configuration.
NodeGroups are deployed to nodes by NodeUser or other permission-consuming resources.

groupName.description: Set the local name for this group. If not provided, the resource name will be used.
groupName.title: Group Name

action.description: Set the action for this entry.
action.title: Action

match.description:
Set the match for this entry. This is a string to match input against - for example "interface" for srl or "configure port" for sros.
Rules here should be specified in the target specific format.
match.title: Match

operatingSystem.description:
Operating system to match against for this rule.
Operating system to deploy this rule to.
operatingSystem.title: Operating System

superuser.description: Make members of this group superusers.
superuser.title: Superuser

privilegeLevel.description: Set the privilege level for this group.
privilegeLevel.title: Privilege Level

spec.title: Specification

nodes.description: List of TopoNodes group has been deployed to.
nodes.title: Nodes

com.nokia.eda.aaa.v1.NodeGroupList.description: NodeGroupList is a list of nodegroups

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-pattern-error: A valid name is made up of one or more components where each component is separated by period characters.  Each component must start and end with a lower case alphanumeric character and the body of the component is made up of zero or more lower case alphanumeric and hyphen characters.

com.nokia.eda.aaa.v1.ServerGroup.description: ServerGroup is the Schema for the servergroups API

x-eda-nokia-com.ui-pattern-error: The apiVersion must be set to aaa.eda.nokia.com/v1
The kind must be set to ServerGroup

spec.description: The ServerGroup defines a list of AAA servers to be used for authentication, authorization, and accounting. It includes settings for both Radius and Tacacs servers, although each ServerGroup is limited to servers of a single type. The ServerGroup is deployed onto nodes using the label selector.

aaaServers.description: The AAA servers that are part of this ServerGroup.

address.description: Address of the AAA server, must be either IPv4 or IPv6, or an FQDN.
address.title: Address

secret.description: Secret used for the AAA server.
secret.title: Secret

aaaServers.title: AAA Servers

nodeGroupSelectors.description: Label selector for NodeGroup resources to deploy on the node for this ServerGroup.
nodeGroupSelectors.title: Node Group Selector

nodeGroups.description: Which NodeGroup resources to deploy on the node for this ServerGroup.
nodeGroups.title: Node Groups

accountingPort.description: Accounting port of the Radius server(s).
accountingPort.title: Accounting Port

authenticationPort.description: Authentication port of the Radius server(s).
authenticationPort.title: Authentication Port

routerKind.description: Kind of the router used to reach the AAA servers.
routerKind.title: Router Kind

privLvlAuthorization.description: Whether to enable TACACS+ privilege level authorization.
privLvlAuthorization.title: Privilege Level Authorization

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds that the node will wait for a response from the AAA server before retrying or continuing to the next server in the group.
timeoutSeconds.title: Timeout (seconds)

type.description: Select the type of all servers in the ServerGroup. Can either be Radius or Tacacs.
type.title: Type

spec.title: Specification

status.description: ServerGroupStatus defines the observed state of ServerGroup.

health.description: Indicates the health score of the ServerGroup. The health score of the ServerGroup is determined by the aggregate health score of all servers in this group on all nodes.
health.title: Health

healthScoreReason.description: Indicates the reason for the health score.
healthScoreReason.title: Health Score Reason

lastChange.description: The time when the state of the resource last changed.
lastChange.title: Last Change

nodes.description: List of nodes this ServerGroup has been applied to
nodes.title: Nodes

operationalState.description: Operational state of the ServerGroup.  The operational state of the ServerGroup is determined by monitoring the operational state of all servers in this group on all nodes.
operationalState.title: Operational State

status.title: Status

com.nokia.eda.aaa.v1.ServerGroupList.description: ServerGroupList is a list of servergroups
